rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 27, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Powers Process Controls' motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to Issues 1 through 4 (Negligence I and Product Liability) and denied as to Issues 5 and 6 (Negligence II). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant is successor-in-interest to Powers Regulator. Statements made during the deposition of Bill Throne preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in contributing to decedent's risk of developing his asbestos-related illness(es). Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 5 and 6. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even assuming that defendant was not in control of the premise(s) at issue, defendant failed to demonstrate this mandates that plaintiffs' Negligence II causes of action fail. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to Issues 1 through 4 because plaintiffs have dismissed those causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ERNESTINE BROWN ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 27, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Paul Brown was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's Separate Statement on this topic fails to raise the sufficiency of plaintiffs' special discovery responses and it violates CRC 3.1350(d)(3). (UMF 17-22.) Regardless, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Monte Manwill preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Additionally, the limited questioning of Mr. Manwill did not demonstrate that he does not have knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claims against defendant. Defendant's arguments related to substantial factor causation fail because such arguments are only based on whether plaintiffs have evidence to demonstrate a threshold exposure. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied, as it is solely based on the same grounds as summary judgment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND COLMAN ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Plaintiffs_ Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 27, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. Even if the Court considers all of defendant's evidence to which plaintiffs object, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that public policy dictates that its motion should be granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
